title: Micro-library restart after school closure
summary: A worst-case routine for keeping children and adults learning when schools, internet, and libraries are unavailable.
content: Micro-library restart after school closure is designed for conditions where normal services, internet, transport, or outside help may be delayed. The practical goal is keeping children and adults learning when schools, internet, and libraries are unavailable. Keep the routine visible, assign one calm recorder, protect vulnerable people first, and change the plan only when conditions or official instructions clearly change.
materials: Notebook or board. Context: “Micro-library restart after school closure”.
Marker or pencil. Context: “Micro-library restart after school closure”.
Tape or labels. Context: “Micro-library restart after school closure”.
Local map or contact list — for «Micro-library restart after school closure»
steps: Write the exact trigger for starting micro-library restart after school closure.
List people, supplies, hazards, and safe locations before action starts. Apply it specifically within the “Micro-library restart after school closure” procedure.
Use the routine to focus on keeping children and adults learning when schools, internet, and libraries are unavailable.
Check vulnerable people first and document unmet needs. Apply it specifically within the “Micro-library restart after school closure” procedure.
Review the plan at fixed times or when official instructions change. Apply it specifically within the “Micro-library restart after school closure” procedure.
warnings: Follow local authorities and qualified responders whenever they are available. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Micro-library restart after school closure”.

Do not improvise with weapons, explosives, dangerous chemicals, medical dosing, or unsafe building entry. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Micro-library restart after school closure”.

Do not let curiosity, filming, rumors, or pride replace distance, shelter, evacuation, hygiene, and records. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Micro-library restart after school closure”.
common_mistakes: Starting too late because the first signs were ignored while carrying out “Micro-library restart after school closure”
Letting many people make conflicting decisions at the same time while carrying out “Micro-library restart after school closure”
Using memory instead of a visible written record while carrying out “Micro-library restart after school closure”
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Micro-library restart after school closure with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Micro-library restart after school closure if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Micro-library restart after school closure and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Micro-library restart after school closure or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Micro-library restart after school closure are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.

The routine ignores clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Micro-library restart after school closure”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Micro-library restart after school closure as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Micro-library restart after school closure when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Micro-library restart after school closure.
short_term: For the first hours of Micro-library restart after school closure, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Micro-library restart after school closure remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Micro-library restart after school closure fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Micro-library restart after school closure to the real environment: check clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
